purpose:
Manages the live set of Server-Sent Events client connections in a `Map<http.ServerResponse, {dashboardFilter}>` and exposes `broadcast`, `addClient`, `removeClient`, `startHeartbeat`, `stopHeartbeat`, and `closeAll` as the single public surface for fan-out. `broadcast(eventName, data)` builds the SSE wire format (`event: <name>
data: <json>

`) once and writes it to every live client; clients with `dashboardFilter` set only receive events whose `data.dashboardId` matches (events without a `dashboardId`, like `dashboards_list` or `queue_changed`, are treated as global and delivered to everyone). On every iteration it lazily reaps clients whose `res.destroyed` or `res.writableEnded` is true and clients whose `res.write` throws — preventing zombie references. The heartbeat (started via `startHeartbeat`, default 15s from `HEARTBEAT_MS`) writes `: ping

` (an SSE comment) to all clients to keep proxies/load balancers from killing idle connections, with the same reap-on-error logic. `closeAll` calls `res.end()` on every client and clears the map — used by graceful shutdown.
gotchas: SSE clients MUST be removed on disconnect or the `sseClients` Map leaks references to dead `http.ServerResponse` objects. The lazy reap inside `broadcast` and the heartbeat is a safety net, but the canonical removal is `req.on('close', () => removeClient(res))` in index.js.

Filtering logic only filters when BOTH `meta.dashboardFilter` AND `eventDashboardId` are truthy — events without a `dashboardId` field bypass the filter entirely (intentional for global events but easy to forget when adding new event types).
`res.write` can throw if the socket is half-closed between the `destroyed` check and the write call — wrapped in try/catch that deletes the client. Don't remove this catch.
`startHeartbeat` does not guard against being called twice — calling it a second time without `stopHeartbeat` would orphan the previous timer.
Heartbeat writes `: ping\n\n` (a comment line) NOT `event: ping`. Browser EventSource silently ignores comments — adding `event:` here would create a phantom `ping` event on the client.
patterns: SSE heartbeat every 15s (HEARTBEAT_MS) to detect disconnects and prevent proxy timeouts.
Per-client metadata (dashboardFilter) stored in the Map value, not the response object — keeps response untouched and enables future per-client options without prototype pollution.
Lazy disconnect reaping on every broadcast iteration — no separate sweep timer.
Global events (no `dashboardId` in payload) bypass filter — `dashboards_list` and `queue_changed` reach all clients regardless of subscription.
conventions: Public function JSDoc comments describe both behavior and parameter shape.
Module-level `let heartbeatTimer = null` is used for the singleton timer reference.
All exports are named — no default export.

related: electron/ipc-handlers.js (IPC bridge mirrors SSE for Electron renderer)
